steps.action: Kpanlogo is a recreational dance and music form with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, grounded.
steps.text: Kpanlogo is a recreational dance and music form with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, grounded.

steps.action: It began in the early 1960s as an innovative dan
steps.text: It began in the early 1960s as an innovative dance form, influenced by American rock and roll, and giving the younger Ga generations a point of distinction from their elders.

steps.action: The kpanlogo dance is often performed low to the
steps.text: The kpanlogo dance is often performed low to the ground, with bent knees and bent back, and frequently features sexually suggestive motions.

steps.action: The music accompanying the kpanlogo dance is dra
steps.text: The music accompanying the kpanlogo dance is drawn from older Ga drumming traditions, such as gome, oge and kolomashie.

steps.action: It is also similar to the "Bo Diddley beat" popu
steps.text: It is also similar to the "Bo Diddley beat" popularized by the U.

steps.action: rhythm and blues musician Bo Diddley.
steps.text: rhythm and blues musician Bo Diddley.

steps.text: "Kpanlogo: A Detailed Description of One Arrangement of a West-African Music and Dance Genre.

steps.action: Accounts of police seizing musical instruments a
steps.text: Accounts of police seizing musical instruments and detaining performers in its early days have been documented.

steps.action: Kpanlogo music uses three types of instruments:
steps.text: Kpanlogo music uses three types of instruments: nono (metal bell), fao (gourd rattle), and kpanlogo drums.

steps.action: Nono plays the key pattern or timeline of the mu
steps.text: Nono plays the key pattern or timeline of the music, supported by the fao.

steps.action: It is common to have three kpanlogo drums in an
steps.text: It is common to have three kpanlogo drums in an ensemble, in the roles of "male voice", "female voice" and "master drum".

steps.action: The main kpanlogo bell part is one of the most c
steps.text: The main kpanlogo bell part is one of the most common and oldest key patterns found in sub-Saharan Africa.

steps.action: The bell pattern used in kpanlogo is the same as
steps.text: The bell pattern used in kpanlogo is the same as the son clave pattern heard in Cuban music and.
